content: Study of H Company's commercial air conditioner final assembly line (PMC10949552). Three sections: final assembly, pre-assembly, testing. 29 stations across two production lines. Workforce: 41 employees (34 assembly workers, 4 inspectors, 2 pre-assembly workers, 2 alternating workers). Activities include welding pipelines, electronic control installation, evacuation, gas injection, and safety testing — some requiring special qualifications. Single 10-hour shift operation. Pre-improvement cycle time: 112.5 seconds. Service time was measured five times per station; average 78.22 seconds, range 33.36 to 152.11 seconds. Hourly output: approximately 32 sets. Improvement results: bottleneck reduced from 96.67 seconds to 74.6 seconds; balance rate improved from 68% to 85%. Methodology: decision tree C4.5 with production data analysis — NOT discrete event simulation.
provenance_note: The paper is directly on-topic by subject (commercial air conditioner assembly line balancing) but its methodology is decision tree machine learning, not DES. Labeled Direct x Adjacent Method — usable for structural and parameter data (29 stations, 41 workers, 112.5 sec cycle, 33-152 sec service range, 10-hour shift, 32 sets/hour, 68%-to-85% balance improvement) as raw context for a DES entry on the same subject, but the paper itself cannot be cited as a DES implementation reference.
labels: pmc, pmc10949552, air-conditioner, assembly-line, decision-tree, adjacent-method, non-des

content: Information aggregated from search-engine snippets describing the Midea washing machine assembly line case study published on automate.org. Midea, the world's largest producer of major appliances, used Visual Components simulation to increase the capacity and flexibility of a high-end washing machine assembly line while reducing costs by 15%. More than 100 components are assembled to produce a high-end washing machine. Results: line balance was improved by 20 percent, reaching more than 90 percent; floor area for the assembly line was reduced by 10 percent; production capacity was increased by 10 percent. The underlying simulation technology is Visual Components, a commercially-available DES and 3D manufacturing simulation platform.
provenance_note: The source URL points to the automate.org case study page but direct fetch returned 403 (commercial/industry association bot-block). All content here was aggregated from multiple search engine result snippets describing the case study. Methodology provenance is DES because the case study describes a Visual Components simulation — a DES implementation — even though the snippets themselves are marketing-style. Recommended for human follow-up via browser access.
labels: midea, washing-machine, visual-components, appliances, automate-org, second-party, 403

content: Information aggregated from multiple search-engine snippets describing an Egyptian home appliance manufacturer's refrigerator assembly line DES case study. No single search result pointed to a specific source URL; content was accumulated from several snippets across research-aggregator sites. A discrete event simulation model was developed to study the performance of a refrigerator assembly line at a leading Egyptian home appliance manufacturer. Refrigerator assembly involves tasks on both the front and the back of the product, making the flow more complex than a one-sided line (often modeled as a two-sided assembly line). The line has 73 workstations. Station 7 was identified as the bottleneck — its work time of 35 seconds exceeds the takt time of the line.
provenance_note: Aggregated from multiple search engine result snippets — no single source URL could be identified from the available metadata. Original publication venue is unclear and would require a library or Google Scholar search to locate. Classified as Second-party x Direct x DES because the case study is explicitly DES even though content was accumulated from second-party snippets. Multi-source snippet aggregation pattern (similar to the GT ISYE 6501 case in the airport security walkthrough).
labels: egyptian, refrigerator, two-sided-assembly, 73-stations, station-7-bottleneck, second-party, multi-source-aggregation
